critical.mandate: Architecture must exist before creating epics
Every story must have depends_on and blocks declarations
Stories must be ordered by dependency topology + business priority

step.title: Load PRD and Architecture
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/prd.md — extract functional requirements
Read {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — extract technical components
GATE: verify architecture.md contains a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section. If missing, HALT — run /kratos-create-arch first to complete adversarial review and architecture refinement.

step.title: Detect Mode
step.action: Check {planning_artifacts}/prd.md header for "Mode: Brownfield"
step.check: Set {mode} = brownfield. Stories must cover gap requirements ONLY — do NOT create stories for existing implemented features.
Set {mode} = greenfield. Create stories for all features from the PRD.

step.title: Define Epics
step.action: Group related features into logical epics
Each epic: name, description, goal, success criteria
Brownfield: epics should focus on gap closure — not existing functionality

step.title: Break Into Stories
step.action: For each epic, create user stories
Each story needs: title, description, acceptance criteria, size estimate (S/M/L/XL)
Use format: "As a [user], I want to [action] so that [benefit]"
Brownfield: stories must trace to PRD gap requirement IDs. Do NOT create stories for existing implemented features.

step.title: Apply Test-Plan Risk Levels
step.action: Read {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md — extract risk assessment: high-risk areas (revenue-critical, security-sensitive, complex logic)
For each story: if it touches a high-risk component, set risk_level: high. Otherwise medium or low.
High-risk stories: add to Dev Notes: "Risk: HIGH — run /kratos-atdd before /kratos-dev-story"

step.title: Declare Dependencies
step.action: For each story, declare depends_on: [story-ids] and blocks: [story-ids]
Ensure no circular dependencies

step.title: Priority Ordering
step.action: Sort stories by: dependency topology first, then business priority
Assign priority: P0 (must-have), P1 (should-have), P2 (nice-to-have)

step.title: Generate Output
Brownfield: Generate Onboarding Knowledge Base
step.action: Skip this step if {mode} is greenfield.
Read the brownfield onboarding template from {installed_path}/../../templates/brownfield-onboarding-template.md

Generate onboarding doc as a knowledge base index linking to ALL artifacts: project-documentation.md, api-documentation.md (if exists), ux-design.md (if exists), event-catalog.md (if exists), dependency-map.md, nfr-assessment.md, performance-test-plan-{date}.md, prd.md, architecture.md, epics-and-stories.md

Include: quick-start guide, project overview, architecture overview, API reference, UX patterns (if frontend), event architecture (if events), dependencies, NFR baselines, performance test plan, gap analysis, stories, conventions, key files
Skip sections for optional artifacts that were not generated
Include a "Reading Order for New Developers" section at the end
step.template-output: Generate developer knowledge base following the brownfield-onboarding-template. Link to every artifact produced by the brownfield workflow. Skip sections for artifacts that were not generated (optional steps that were skipped). Include quick-start, architecture summary, conventions, key files, and reading order.

step.title: Optional: Edge Case Analysis
step.ask: Would you like to hunt for edge cases in the stories? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended to catch gaps before sprint planning. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/tasks/review-edge-case-hunter.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: edge case analysis can be run anytime later with /kratos-edge-cases

step.title: Optional: Adversarial Review
step.ask: Would you like to run an adversarial review on the epics and stories? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended before sprint planning. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: adversarial review can be run anytime later with /kratos-adversarial
